name: Cluster 2: HOW (Technical Feasibility)
description: Evaluates the experimental approach, methods rigor, and specific aims structure
mcp_condition: Only invoke ChEMBL and ClinicalTrials MCPs when the research domain (from input/direction.md) involves drug targets, compounds, or clinical interventions. For computational, ecological, or other non-pharmacological domains, skip these MCPs and evaluate feasibility based on intrinsic method quality, preliminary data, and published literature alone.
grounding_instructions: If domain-appropriate: use ChEMBL to verify target druggability and known bioactivity; use ClinicalTrials to check existing trial landscape and identify competitive or complementary studies. Always verify that aims are independent (project survives if one aim fails) and that methods are established or supported by preliminary data.

scores.1: No approach evaluation; methods not assessed
scores.2: Superficial methods review; misses major design issues
scores.3: Adequate review; identifies some approach weaknesses
scores.4: Good review of approach with attention to controls, statistics, pitfalls
scores.5: Comprehensive approach review; ensures feasibility, rigor, alternatives for each aim

criteria.name: Specific Aims Structure
criteria.description: Quality of specific aims page evaluation and guidance

scores.2: Superficial aims review; structure issues unaddressed
scores.3: Adequate review; identifies main aims structure issues
scores.4: Good review ensuring aims are testable and independent
scores.5: Excellent aims review; complete structure optimization with specific guidance on independence, testability, and logical flow
